question: Which priority policy and slot-allocation strategy maximizes net frontier advancement under current demand?

answer.policy: value_density UCB1 (quality = merge_rate * (1 + log1p(lessons)) + UCB1 exploration term)
answer.slot_allocation: single-DOMEX focus per session, 100% expert utilization
answer.quality_gate: EAD enforcement (expect/actual/diff mandatory at MERGED closure)
answer.campaign_mechanism: structural mode enforcement (--mode required for 2nd+ wave lanes)

retrospective_backtest.finding: value_density rho=0.792 (p<0.001) ONLY positive correlate. fifo=-0.46, risk_first=-0.39, hybrid=-0.29, ucb1=-0.14

integration.finding: UCB1 exploit replaced with value_density. Top-10 quality +80%, merge +48%

false_regression.root_cause: close_lane.py 2 bugs: archive search gap + substitution failure (L-747)

post_fix_large.finding: True merge 93.5% (raw 80.6%, 5/7 ABANDONED had actual= populated). EAD 90.5% recent. 15 domains. 23 frontiers advanced

mode_enforcement.finding: 0% voluntary adoption → structural ERROR gate. 4/4 test cases pass

wave_planner_accuracy.finding: mode= adoption 84% post-build. COMMIT follow-through 100% for in-pipeline domains. Advisory gap for cold domains (L-794)

residual_questions: Does value_density remain optimal as domain landscape changes? (monitor, not test)
Full explore→harden→resolve traversal rate still 0% (F-STR3 territory)
Advisory gap for cold domains (L-794) — wave planner diagnoses but cannot dispatch
